Solving the Schrodinger Equation¶

This document describes the math of the SchrodingerSolver.

Basic 1-D problem on a non-uniform mesh¶

Read Tan_1990

The kinetic term is tri-diagonal and symmetric. For each row \(\sigma\), the center term is

\[T_{\sigma,\sigma}=\frac{\hbar^2}{2dz_{\sigma}}\left(\frac{1}{m_{z,\sigma-.5}dz_{\sigma-.5}}+\frac{1}{m_{z,\sigma+.5}dz_{\sigma+.5}}\right)\]

And the left and right off-diagonal terms are respectively

\[T_{\sigma, \sigma-1}=\frac{\hbar^2}{2m_{z,\sigma-.5} dz_{\sigma-.5} \sqrt{dz_{\sigma-1}dz_{\sigma}}},\quad T_{\sigma, \sigma+1}=\frac{\hbar^2}{2m_{z,\sigma+.5} dz_{\sigma+.5} \sqrt{dz_{\sigma+1}dz_{\sigma}}}\]
